title: Iliad, Book 4: The Breaking of the Truce
description: Book 4 of Homer's Iliad: The Breaking of the Truce.
introduction: Book 4 of the Iliad: The Breaking of the Truce. The Greek text is from the Venetus A manuscript (10th century), the oldest complete manuscript of the Iliad.

translation.original_text: Οἱ δὲ θεοὶ πὰρ Ζηνὶ καθήμενοι ἠγορόωντο χρυσέῳ ἐν δαπέδῳ.
translation.text: The gods sat with Zeus on the golden floor, and among them Hebe poured nectar. Zeus spoke to Hera, provoking her with taunting words about Troy. But Athena guided an arrow from Pandarus that broke the truce, and the battle was renewed.
